threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You repeatedly ask for directions to the 'High Street' instead of just saying 'the road' like everyone else. It's amusing, really.
Last night on the 35, a guy serenaded the bus with 'Wonderwall' while juggling apples. The driver just laughed and kept his eyes on the road.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I stepped out on a rainy Tuesday, and a whole bus shelter was repurposed as an art installation. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you find spontaneous dance parties in abandoned buildings after a BVG strike. It’s a unique blend of chaos and creativity.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You hear ‘that’s what she said’ way more often in the local coffee shops than anywhere else. It’s like a secret handshake for the true locals.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Yesterday, I saw a guy do a backflip onto the bus just as the doors were closing. The driver smirked but didn’t wait for him to catch his breath.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think it's all about the parks and plazas, but the real vibe happens in the narrow streets at 2 am when locals stumble home from the bars.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell they’re new by the way they still stare at the metro map like it's a treasure map with no clues.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think being late for the tube means missing it. Here, it means you’ve got 20 minutes to kill at the pub.
Caught a bloke on the 47 last night passionately debating with the driver about whether the 53 ran later. Only in Bermondsey, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I realized I was home when I spotted a family of ducks waddling down Sonnenallee, completely unbothered by the traffic. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, you can find a spontaneous open-air film screening at a park almost every weekend, complete with local snacks. Totally a Berlin vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know you can't trust the BART schedule on a Friday night—it's like playing roulette with your commute.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a tourist trying to board a crowded 38 Geary with a huge suitcase, completely ignoring the 'No Bikes' sign. Classic San Francisco moment.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists never notice that locals swap traditional paella for a quick plat de fideuà—much better with the dry noodles and seafood in the bars here.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can usually tell by the way they exclusively order tapas without knowing to ask for 'para picar' first.
